programmes.show_name: Romesh Ranganathan: For the Love of Hip Hop
programmes.show_description: Celebrating hip hop's women with some exclusive Maida Vale session tracks.
Tony celebrates International Women's Day with a selection of music from the greatest Queens of Soul - including classics from Aretha Franklin, Diana Ross, Jaki Graham and more!
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1985.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1998.

programmes.show_name: Neil Sedaka: The Music of My...
programmes.show_description: ...Life - Johnnie Walker Meets Neil Sedaka. In tribute to Neil Sedaka who has sadly passed away, another chance to hear him in conversation with Johnnie Walker at the BBC Radio Theatre.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from Frank Ifield, The Chiffons and The Rockin' Berries. This week, the top three comes from 1963.
Adeel Akhtar joins Dermot to chat about his new West End role. Plus, we mark 90 years of the Spitfire. And of course The Mystery Voice.
Radio 2's very own Fearne Cotton joins Joel live in the studio to chat about her latest book, 'Likeable'.
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma and enjoy a menu full of your favourite music, great conversation and a delicious dive into the BBC archives for dessert.
Rylan is joined by The Appleton sisters for a very special KaRYoke, plus Mummy Linda's favourite author Kimberley Chambers pops by to talk about her new book.
Shaun Keaveny sits in with fabulous teatime tunes and your tales of the extraordinary and the everyday.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 80s with Gary Davies
programmes.show_description: Sananda Maitreya shares his favourite tracks on My 80s and there's a Soul Sistas Mastermix.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Fearne's remembering some great 90s telly moments, including Airline, Daria, and the revival of The Wombles! It's all soundtracked by the likes of *NSYNC, Kula Shaker and M People.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Yeehaw! Fearne's got an hour of tunes from the likes of Shania Twain, Billy Ray Cyrus, LeAnn Rimes, Dolly Parton, Rednex, and so much more!
2/3. In episode two, fearless women who pushed boundaries, from Emmylou Harris to Dolly Parton, Reba McEntire to The Chicks.
James Morrison introduces music from Gunhild Carling, Maynard Ferguson, Cindy Bradley, Kai Winding, Oli Parker, and Night Auras, plus covers of Taylor Swift and George Michael.
In celebration of her 80th birthday the iconic singer and Oscar winning actress talks about her family, famous friends and colleagues, good and bad fortune and incredible career.
Tony Blackburn, begins with a look at the groundbreaking 60s, where Tina Turner, Grace Slick, Nico, PP Arnold and Janis Joplin led the charge when it came to rock vocals.
Bob Harris takes us through the 70s, focusing on the rise of rock giants like Stevie Nicks, Pattie Smith, Suzi Quatro, Christine McVie and Debbie Harry, who influenced a generation.
Emmylou Harris and The Nash Ramblers performing at London's Royal Festival Hall in 1991. Featuring Two More Bottles of Wine, Together Again and C'est La Vie (You Never Can Tell).
The Charlatans' frontman and solo artist Tim Burgess joins Vernon Kay to choose the songs that have soundtracked his life so far.
Rose Ayling-Ellis, actress, presenter and former winner of Strictly Come Dancing, reflects on her lifelong love of reading.
Your stories, dedications and classic love songs with Michael... easy like Sunday morning.
Paddy's live from Salford with a new Timewarp for you to finish, the Weekest Link quiz and your Sunday Dinners.
Elaine's got a sensational sequence of female-led show tunes for International Women's Day, from musicals including Waitress, Made in Dagenham, Come From Away and Sister Act.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 70s with Bob Harris
programmes.show_description: Bob marks International Women's Day with some of the decade's greatest female artists. His 'Best of the Test' comes from Bonnie Raitt on the Old Grey Whistle Test in 1976.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1976.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1980.
Tony Blackburn spins his personal pick of the hits from across the decades. To listen on most smart speakers, just 'ask BBC Sounds to play Tony Blackburn's Golden Hour'.
We celebrate the music of Sir George Martin which includes The Beatles, Cilla and Bernard Cribbens. Plus, a selection of singing drummers, so expect some Monkees, Eagles and Ringo.
Angela Griffin marks International Women's Day with a playlist of influential female voices. Featuring Destiny's Child, Deniece Williams, Toni Braxton and Carly Simon.

programmes.show_name: Radio 2 Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin celebrates inspirational female artists with an All Queens Unwinds Mix. Featuring Adele, SWV, Tracy Chapman and Whitney Houston.
